MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Assigned To Trial Court

Motion To Reopen Discovery For Sole Purpose Of Deposition Of Officer Kevin Horan
Off Calendar Oct-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michelle Weiss, Esq. (415-466-8717) of Bay Area Bicycle Law for Plaintiff.
James Hannawalt, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3913) of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff's Counsel reports she is willing to work around the schedule of Defense Counsel. (as fully reported on the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS TAAKE's Motion To Reopen Discovery For Sole Purpose Of Deposition Of Officer Kevin Horan.

Plaintiff Nicholas Taake's motion to reopen discovery for the sole purpose of the deposition of Officer Kevin Horan is granted. Mr. Taake has shown good cause for both his request to reopen discovery and to take the deposition of Sergeant Horan. Mr. Taake has shown that he made reasonable efforts to depose Sergeant Horan prior to the close of discovery and that Officer Horan may possess information that is either relevant or is likely to lead to the discovery or admissible information. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Lewis Gillian, CSR No:12355, email: lgillian@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION To Quash The Deposition Subpoena Of Sfpd Sgt Kevin Horan And For Monetray Sanctions And Proof Of Service And Exhibit A
Off Calendar Oct-20-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michelle Weiss, Esq. (415-466-8717) of Bay Area Bicycle Law for Plaintiff.
James Hannawalt, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3913) of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff's Counsel reports she is willing to work around the schedule of Defense Counsel. (as fully reported on the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION To Quash The Deposition Subpoena Of Sfpd Sgt Kevin Horan And For Monetray Sanctions

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to quash the deposition subpoena of SFPD Sergeant Horan and for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiff Nicholas Taake has shown good cause for both his request to reopen discovery and to take the deposition of Sergeant Horan. Mr. Taake has shown that he made reasonable efforts to depose Sergeant Horan prior to the close of discovery and that Officer Horan may possess information that is either relevant or is likely to lead to the discovery or admissible information. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Lewis Gillian, CSR No:12355, email: lgillian@sftc.org. (D302)
